title: helianthemum croceum subsp. croceum
intro: Helianthemum croceum subsp. croceum, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial plant species in the Cistaceae family, characterized by its pink to yellow flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Helianthemum croceum subsp. croceum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
